title: Memory delete glitch can cause unintended data exposure / information leak
what_failed: The broad delete that wiped 20 entries (T-140) is also a potential info leak vector — if wrong entries get deleted and re-written with incorrect data, or if deleted entries surface in unexpected ways, sensitive profile data could be mishandled.
suggested_fix: Audit all memory_delete call paths. Add confirmation step for bulk deletes (>3 entries). Log all deletes with entry snapshots to a write-ahead log before deletion. Tie into T-140 fix.
resolution: Info-leak vector from mass delete closed by: bulk guard (>3 → force=True), WAL snapshot before deletion, soft-delete (invalid_at keeps rows recoverable), fast-path non-match filter.
